spec.reasoning: The task references 'row 11' explicitly in the instruction and answer position, so any blank_row_insert or title_row_insert would shift rows and break the task (as seen in the failed Attempt 1). Instead, I use two safe perturbation strategies: (1) Renaming 'Employee Name' to 'Staff Name' — this is a semantically equivalent rename at the same descriptiveness level. The header 'Employee Name' appears as both a header and a data value in A5, so a data_value_rename is included. I do NOT rename 'Jhon Smith' since it also appears as a header and data overlap, and the instruction doesn't reference it. (2) Adding a distractor column 'Day Shift Ratio' after the last column — this is plausibly related to the night shift percentage task but clearly different, testing whether the model gets confused by a similar-sounding column. The instruction says 'row 11' and 'B11:M11' which remain valid since no rows or relevant columns are shifted. I set instruction_paraphrase to false per the rules.
